decisions.title: Configctl remains the sole owner of DSL lifecycle and compilation
decisions.description: Language expansion stays anchored in configctl draft, validate, compile, and activate flows. Validator, consumer, emulator, server, and raccoon-cli remain downstream of compiled runtime truth rather than becoming alternate authors of language semantics.
alternatives: Let validator infer unsupported language capabilities directly from config payloads
Move capability gating into raccoon-cli or server-facing contracts

decisions.title: DSL expansion remains declarative, per-record, and fail-closed
decisions.description: The next language step is limited to deterministic per-payload semantics that can be validated, compiled, projected, executed, and diagnosed explicitly. Stateful correlation, temporal windows, aggregates, external lookups, and arbitrary expression power are deferred.
alternatives: Open the DSL to generalized expression evaluation early
Compensate for missing semantics with ad hoc runtime logic in validator

decisions.title: Projection, result, incident, and query contracts evolve additively with explainability
decisions.description: Any richer rule support must ship with additive projection metadata, runtime-facing contracts, and operational explainability. The repository will not accept silent contract mutation or payload inflation that hides compatibility risk from server, validator, or raccoon-cli surfaces.
alternatives: Mutate projection and result shapes implicitly as operators are added
Keep explainability in logs only and leave contracts unchanged

decisions.title: Platform consolidation is centered on canonical workflow and external guard rails
decisions.description: Block 8 is not a new architecture layer. It strengthens raccoon-cli analyzers, smoke, trace-pack, results-inspect, baselines, and docs so the engineering loop around the runtime becomes more predictable, auditable, and drift-resistant.
alternatives: Treat platform consolidation as mostly documentation work
Add new runtime governance endpoints instead of hardening the existing workflow

decisions.title: Compiled artifacts now carry explicit DSL execution capabilities
decisions.description: The first execution slice of Blocks 7-8 derives normalized capabilities from the validated config document, stores them in the compilation artifact, propagates them through runtime projections and compact bootstrap summaries, and keeps the runtime truth anchored in configctl-owned compile metadata.
alternatives: Infer supported rule operators only from validator code paths
Leave capability metadata implicit in schema_version and runtime_loader alone

decisions.title: Validator execution now fails closed on capability mismatch
decisions.description: Validator evaluation now checks projected rule operators against the compiled artifact capability envelope before rule execution. Unsupported operators return a conflict instead of silently behaving as if support exists, which keeps future DSL growth bounded by compile-time truth.
alternatives: Continue treating validator source code as the only capability gate
Emit best-effort violations for unsupported operators and proceed
